The '''deliberative''' mood is used to question whether an action should take place. It is marked by the suffix '''-yam''' in the future tense and '''-nkyam''' in the hodiernal tense.

== Overview ==

The deliberative expresses a future statement while questioning whether the action ought to happen. It is generally equivalent to a yes/no question about the desirability or appropriateness of an action.

== Suffixes ==

{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Tense !! Suffix
|-
| Future || ''-yam''
|-
| Hodiernal || ''-nkyam''
|}

== Usage ==

: '''Thozharanyam''' — "Should I eat?"

The deliberative frames the statement as a question of whether the action is advisable. The question particle may be added for greater explicitness, but it is optional; the deliberative suffix alone is sufficient to convey the questioning force.

[[Category:evidentialities]]
